content: We can frame Self-Refine’s no-external-data iterative improvement framework as a base structural backbone that self-reflection mechanisms can plug into, creating a multi-perspective self-refinement workflow that keeps Self-Refine’s core advantage of needing no extra training or supervision. This hybrid system would resolve Self-Refine’s blind spot of single-perspective feedback by leveraging the inconsistent, multiple-view checks from LLM self-reflection mechanisms while avoiding the common need for additional labeled data in many existing reflection methods.
